text.title: Ruby on Rails Service Objects Core Rules

rule.statement: Each service class MUST encapsulate exactly one business action and expose exactly one public `#call` method (or `.call` class-method). Multi-action services are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Single-action services make naming explicit + testing focused; multi-action services drift back into god-objects.

rule.statement: Service `#call` MUST return a ServiceResult (or equivalent success/failure value object). Returning naked booleans / nil / exceptions for failure is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Explicit result type makes success/failure paths visible in callers; exceptions for control flow break Ruby idiom.

rule.statement: Services that perform multi-write business operations MUST wrap them in `ActiveRecord::Base.transaction`. Controllers and models MUST NOT open transactions for business actions.
rule.rationale: Service is the unit-of-work boundary; controller-tx blends HTTP + DB; model-tx breaks single-responsibility.

rule.statement: Models MUST NOT call services from callbacks. Services MUST be called from controllers / jobs / other services.
rule.rationale: Calling service from callback creates implicit graphs of side effects triggered by `.touch` / fixtures.

rule.statement: Every service MUST have a unit test that runs without booting Rails HTTP stack. Service tests MUST NOT use feature/system-test runners.

rationale.source: RSpec Rails reference — request specs vs unit specs

rule.rationale: Isolation = speed + focus; feature tests for HTTP-level behavior only.
